attractions.n: Hilltop Haven Wine and Farm Market
attractions.s: Local wine & farm market, live piano, cheese and soaps.
3-level mall with arcade, food court, carousel.
Indoor playground; private parties.
Bowling, mini golf, arcade, laser tag under one roof.
Walking trails, picnic areas, gazebo.
Short wooded loop trail tucked between neighborhoods.
Indoor trampoline & adventure park.
Top-ranked private club (members/events).
Fresh produce, deli, butcher, bulk goods.
Historic farmstead site, soccer field, walking trail.
Craft brewery in Hatboro, live music, dive-bar charm.
Hand-crafted Gothic-Romanesque cathedral; free tours.
Medieval art & religious history in a 1930s Romanesque castle home.
Animal sanctuary with workshops and farm tours.
1928 Glenside gem hosting national touring acts.
Saturday-morning market: meats, produce, baked goods, honey (Apr–Nov).
Horsham's 1722 Keith House — the only surviving colonial PA governor's residence.
Abington's 250-acre gem: pond, mini golf & trails.
9-hole course, gym, indoor + outdoor pools.
Hiking trails, cave, rock climbing, picnic pavilions along the Pennypack.
Historic aircraft at the former NAS-JRB Willow Grove.
Well-maintained private course, scenic wooded holes.
Alice-in-Wonderland-themed brewery; board games, karaoke, open mic.
Creative small-batch beer in Huntingdon Valley; darts, live music.
Colonial mansion; self-guided tours Sundays 1–4.
4DX & IMAX screens, modern amenities.
Public course with banquet venue & bar/grill.
Nonprofit 1928 art-house triplex anchoring walkable Ambler.
Creek-side trails, stone crossings, old-growth trees; links to Green Ribbon Trail.
Hiking, picnic areas, hawk-watch observation deck.
Cobblestoned Germantown Ave: galleries, gardens & cafés.
Vegan-friendly brewpub with wood-fired pizza, beer garden, live music.
Penn's 92-acre Victorian arboretum: fernery, canopy walk, garden railway.
Rev-War site where Washington first met Lafayette; monthly colonial cooking demos.
Philadelphia-area art collection in a restored mansion; sculpture garden.
Elegant estate winery in Blue Bell; Fri–Sun tastings.
55 free acres of English-landscape grounds in Germantown.
Reclining seats, multiple screens.
Farm market, flowers, hayrides, black-cherry ice cream.
1767 mansion at the center of the Battle of Germantown.
Farm animals, pick-your-own flowers, produce, ice cream. Free to visit.
12-screen theater inside Plymouth Meeting Mall.
1,800 acres of gorge trails, Forbidden Drive & a covered bridge in city limits.
PA's biggest casino floor, racing & concerts in Bensalem.
Rides, 4D cinema & a mini-Philly built from a million bricks.
Canal-side strip of indie shops, restaurants & the towpath.
Covered bridge, Neshaminy Creek, horse trails, sledding hills.
Highly rated themed escape rooms.
Grand Victorian garden cemetery with skyline views and history tours.
Themed rooms in Lansdale; friendly game masters.
Six-story concrete castle stuffed with 50,000 early-American tools.
Mid-size conservation-focused zoo in Norristown.
Bucks County impressionists in a repurposed 1884 prison.
Free 6.5-acre playground with the famous 1905 giant wooden slide.
Free caroler-figurine museum & Christmas emporium in Chalfont.
Working 1912 tile factory; watch handmade Mercer tiles being made.
Henry Mercer's 44-room hand-built concrete castle, tile-covered ceilings.
1744 Pennsylvania-German farmstead; free tours, craft demos.
Hands-on children's museum in Memorial Hall with a 1908 carousel.
Japanese house, koi pond & tea ceremonies in Fairmount Park.
Hauntingly preserved 1829 prison; Al Capone's cell; Halloween Nights.
Iconic lit boathouses; riverside biking and rowing regattas.
America's first zoo: 1,300 animals and Zoo360 overhead animal trails.
World-class collection across 2,000 years; the Rocky Steps.
Largest Rodin collection outside Paris, The Thinker out front.
Staggering wall-to-wall Renoirs, Cézannes & Matisses hung salon-style.
Mini golf, carousel & playground on one of Penn's original five squares.
Science museum: planetarium, giant walk-through heart, Baldwin locomotive.
Dinosaur hall, classic dioramas & live butterflies since 1812.
Artist studios, markets & garden hangout on a Delaware River pier.
America's oldest continuously inhabited street (1703).
Interactive constitutional history; Signers' Hall bronze statues.

attractions.n: National Shrine of Our Lady of Czestochowa
attractions.s: Hilltop Polish-American shrine with sweeping stained glass.
The upholsterer's home where the first-flag story began.
Legendary 1893 indoor market: Amish bakers, roast pork sandwiches, 80+ vendors.
60+ interactive optical-illusion exhibits in Old City.
The cracked icon of American independence; free, no tickets needed.
Famed medical-history museum: anatomical specimens & oddities.
Sesame Street rides, water park & character parades in Langhorne.
Birthplace of the Declaration and Constitution; free timed tours.
Washington's actual war tent headlines this immersive Rev-War museum.
Hippos, sharks & touch tanks on the Camden waterfront.
World-class archaeology: 15-ton Egyptian sphinx, Maya, Mesopotamia.
Isaiah Zagar's dazzling mosaic labyrinth on South Street.
Tour the most decorated US battleship, bow to engine room.

attractions.n: The Escape Game King of Prussia
attractions.s: Polished multi-room escape games at the KOP mall.
Year-round farm fun: pick-your-own, fall fest, holiday light show.
Top-reviewed escape rooms; private bookings.
70 shops & seasonal festivals in a colonial-style village.
One of America's largest malls: 450 stores under linked roofs.

attractions.n: iFLY Indoor Skydiving King of Prussia
attractions.s: Bodyflight wind tunnel — skydiving without the plane.
Multiple tasting rooms; weekend food trucks & live music.
Oldest surviving botanic garden in North America; free, on the Schuylkill.
8 miles of Skippack Creek; wooded loop trails, horse trails.
Three-story driving-range bays with food & games.
Walkable strip of boutiques, galleries & BYOBs.
134 acres of native PA wildflowers near Washington Crossing.
Free 42-acre public garden on a Villanova estate.
New 2026 indoor play space in Dublin Town Center: open play and imaginative zones for ages 0–6.
Home of the Phillies; ballpark tours on non-game days.
Where Washington crossed the Delaware, Christmas 1776; annual reenactment.
Flyers & Sixers arena plus major touring concerts.

attractions.n: John James Audubon Center at Mill Grove
attractions.s: Audubon's first American home; bird museum & trails.
75 legendary racing sports cars; monthly demo days.
Hands-on New Hope museum reopened after a 2026 sensory-friendly renovation: six new exhibits incl. a covered bridge and village square.
A 'pleasure garden' many rank among America's most beautiful; Wed–Sun.
Go-karts, bowling, laser tag & arcade in Oaks.
Tram ride through a warehouse of nickelodeons, classic cars & pop-culture oddities.
Storied 1939 gristmill theater on the Delaware.
Vintage steam & diesel excursions through Bucks countryside.
Twin river towns: galleries, antiques, canal walks, a footbridge between states.
Revolutionary War encampment; visitor center, drive/bike loop, free entry.
Free woodland garden ablaze with azaleas & rhododendrons.
New for 2026: a brewery-tavern in the old Point Pleasant firehouse along the Delaware.
Tidal-marsh boardwalks & bald eagles minutes from the airport; free.
1777 siege fort still intact; casemates, cannons & ghost lore.
Closest ski hill to Philly; summer zip-line canopy tours.
Expansive hiking trails, historic buildings, creek access.
42 surreal acres of monumental sculpture & roaming peacocks in Hamilton.
650 acres: champion trees, treehouses & 17 miles of trails.
Montco's biggest park: 814-ac reservoir, trails, boat rentals.
1,450-acre lake for boating & biking below Haycock Mountain.
Quakertown food hall in a restored trolley barn: vendors, craft-beverage bar, fire pits, indoor/outdoor events Thu–Sun.
The wildly sculptural hilltop studio of the 'dean of American craftsmen.'
300-acre family farm: pick-your-own, hayrides, big farm market.
Philadelphia Union MLS soccer on the Chester riverfront.
35+ helicopters; climb aboard several. Kid heaven.
Gothic campus strolls; landmark new art museum opened 2025.
Boulder field that chimes like bells when tapped — bring a hammer.
Three generations of Wyeths in a riverside gristmill.
Family-run crystal cavern tours since 1930 in Hellertown.
Saturday-night pro rodeo running since 1929 — the East's oldest.
535-acre lake: sailing, kayaking, sunsets over the hills.
Quirky 1800s ironworks village above French Creek boulders; bakery & inn.
Flagship Downingtown brewpub of the HopDevil makers.
Skiing & snow tubing plus year-round resort dining and spa.
A Versailles-style château & gardens in Wilmington.
du Pont's 175-room house of American antiques + storybook garden.
Restored 1771–1883 iron plantation; orchard & casting demos.
World-famous horticultural display; illuminated fountain shows.
Casino, shops & shows inside the old steel mill.
Howard Pyle illustrations & Pre-Raphaelite treasures.
Smithsonian-affiliated machines-and-steel story next to the stacks.
Arts campus beneath Bethlehem Steel's monumental blast furnaces.
Camping, glamping, two lakes, extensive wooded trails.
Original du Pont gunpowder works along the Brandywine.
UNESCO-listed 1741 Moravian quarter; Christmas City charm.
Major coasters plus a drive-through safari in Jackson.
Hands-on science in downtown Allentown's new 2024 building.
Birthplace of the frontiersman; 579 bucolic acres.
Coasters plus a huge included water park in Allentown.
Ravishing native-plant garden; famous spring wildflower slopes.
75+ vehicles from horseless carriages to hot rods.
Four floors of crayon-themed creativity in Easton.
2,700-acre free eco-estate: orchid range, eagle cam, bike loops.
Pine Barrens bog-iron town frozen in 1876, with a mansion, sawmill, and trailheads.
1908 Japanese-style pagoda with 30-mile views over Reading.
Compact zoo inside a game preserve; giraffe feedings.
PA's most-visited show cave — crystal formations toured since 1871.
Art, science & a planetarium in a 25-acre arboretum.
Flying WWII warbirds incl. a rare P-61 Black Widow restoration.
PA's biggest vertical; summer biking & aerial ropes courses.
Berks County's best view — a rocky 9-mile Appalachian Trail loop out of Hamburg.
Living-history 1830s ironmaking village + Pine Creek Railroad.
40 shops around a jam & relish kitchen in Intercourse.
Destination outdoor megastore: mountain of taxidermy, big aquarium.
Broadway-scale biblical spectaculars with live animals.
Famous 5-acre corn maze & 50 farm activities.
Baymen's boatworks, decoy carvers, and a lighthouse replica on stilts over the marsh.
100+ historic locomotives under one colossal roof.
America's oldest operating railroad; steam through Amish farms.
Stark medieval-looking dormitories of a 1730s celibate religious commune, remarkably intact.
Restored 18th-century village turned shopping green, with a lake loop and carousel.
Point Pleasant classic: rides, aquarium, funnel cake.
Hydrus coaster hanging over the surf, a 1910 carousel, and a waterpark next door.
Beloved fairy-tale park for little kids since 1955.
World-famous raptor lookout; 18,000 hawks & eagles pass each fall.
Penguins, harbor seals, and sharks beside a mile of rides, arcades, and beach.

attractions.n: Delaware Water Gap National Recreation Area
attractions.s: 40 river miles where the Delaware slices through the ridge — hike, paddle, or just gawk.
Cliff-framed river gap: Mt. Tammany hike, beaches, waterfalls.
Rides & pier games of Jersey Shore fame.
Ten untouched miles of dunes — NJ's wildest ocean beach.

attractions.n: Edwin B. Forsythe NWR Wildlife Drive
attractions.s: Eight-mile dike road through salt marsh; egrets, ospreys, and the AC skyline on the horizon.
Guided farmhouse tours & countryside bus rides since 1955.
Hundreds of pinball machines on free play — pay by the hour, rain or shine.
Music-city beach town: Stone Pony, Silverball arcade, murals.
A 'kingdom for kids' — gentle coasters & Duke the Dragon.
Open-window vintage train up the whitewater gorge.
1861 Italianate railroad-baron house, furnishings untouched since the family left.
'Switzerland of America': Victorian streets in the Lehigh Gorge.
140-year-old vaudeville hall, under 500 seats, famously good acoustics.
Climb 'Old Barney' for Long Beach Island panoramas.
1871 lockup where the Molly Maguires hanged; the handprint on cell 17 still won't wash off.
Clear mountain reservoir above Jim Thorpe with a beach, boat rentals, and ridge trails.
6,000 acres of lake and trail; late February brings tens of thousands of snow geese.
A living Pennsylvania German village of 40 buildings with working trades and heirloom seeds.
Flat rail-trail along whitewater and cliffs; bike White Haven down to Jim Thorpe.
America's first commercial pretzel bakery, 1861 — twist your own on the 20-minute tour.
Oldest continuously operating public market in America (1730).
Beaches, bike path & Fort Hancock at the shore's northern tip.
Pennsylvania's largest outdoor waterpark — 37 slides down the face of a ski mountain.
The Poconos' biggest ski hill, plus a mountain coaster, zip lines, and indoor waterpark.
Drive to the 2,133-ft summit of Camelback for a three-state view and rim trails.
Twelve-mile auto tour over tidal flats; snow geese by the thousand in late fall.
Ski slopes, alpine coaster & a giant indoor waterpark.
Edison's West Orange lab and Glenmont estate, machines and all, largely as he left them.
84-degree indoor waterpark built for kids, with day passes when rooms allow.
Africa-themed indoor waterpark with day passes.
A six-story tin elephant from 1881 you can climb inside — America's oldest roadside attraction.
New Jersey's tallest light, 228 steps up, with the casino skyline laid out below.
Ferris wheel 227 feet over the Atlantic, with go-karts and funnel cake behind it.
River-loop trails, a working 1794 grist mill and reliable bald eagles.
The original boardwalk: casinos, Steel Pier, salt water taffy.
Squat 1827 tower where the Susquehanna finally gives up and becomes the Bay.
America's Greatest Family Resort — 2.5 dry-town miles of rides & fudge.
The wooden Cyclone, the Wonder Wheel, a boardwalk, and a hot dog older than the subway.
'Niagara of Pennsylvania' — eight falls linked by boardwalks.
16,000 acres of trout streams, hemlock trails, and a swimming beach at Sand Spring Lake.
One ferry, two islands: crown views over the harbor and the immigration hall in the ledgers.
Free tours of America's oldest brewery (1829), aging caves included.
Hands-on science in Jersey City under the largest planetarium dome in the Western Hemisphere.

attractions.n: Liberty State Park & Statue of Liberty Ferry
attractions.s: Skyline lawns; ferries to Lady Liberty & Ellis Island.
A 16-acre ice-age rubble plain with no soil and no trees — walk out onto the moon.
The only intact charcoal blast furnace in the Western Hemisphere, cannons cast here for 1776.
An intact 1850s company patch town, later the film set for "The Molly Maguires."
102 floors up the tallest tower in the hemisphere, with the harbor laid out like a map.
Twin reflecting pools & the moving story beneath.
Mount Hope Estate turns Elizabethan weekends mid-August to late October — joust included.
Walk the elevated promenade at sunrise; the Gothic stone towers and cable web do the rest.
Cherry esplanade in spring, a serious bonsai collection, and a Japanese hill-and-pond garden.
Indoor coasters, an indoor water park, and an indoor ski slope in the Meadowlands. Rain-proof.
Elevated rail-trail park through Chelsea.
The 86th-floor open-air deck still beats every newer rival for sheer Art Deco romance.
A WWII carrier moored on the Hudson, with a Concorde, a submarine, and a space shuttle aboard.
Art-deco icon with the 86th-floor open-air deck.
Beaux-Arts cathedral of commuting: green ceiling sky, brass clock, whispering gallery below.
Free hangar of cargo giants at Dover AFB — climb aboard a C-5 nose and a C-141.
The one deck with the Empire State Building in the photo; Central Park fills the other side.
The neon crossroads of the world.
Starry Night, Campbell's Soup Cans, and Les Demoiselles within a few galleries of each other.
Make-your-own-flavor ice cream lab in Columbia.
12,000 timepieces in Columbia, anchored by the astronomical 1878 Engle Clock.
Dinos, the blue whale & the dazzling Gilder Center.
Two million works — America's greatest museum.
843 acres of the world's most famous city park.
130-foot free-falling cascade at the end of a flat third-mile boardwalk. Free.
Free admission, 550-plus animals, and a giraffe-and-zebra savanna that punches above its size.
Salt-marsh boardwalk, terrapin rescue tanks, and the best back-bay birding in Stone Harbor.

attractions.n: Pioneer Tunnel Coal Mine & Steam Train
attractions.s: Open mine cars roll 1,800 feet into Mahanoy Mountain; a steam lokie runs the ridge above.
A WWII dive-bomber training hangar packed with warbirds, on a working airport.
Medieval Europe reassembled on a bluff over the Hudson, unicorn tapestries included.
Free factory-tour ride, tastings & candy-making add-ons.
Two lakes on the Pocono plateau ringed by 12,000 acres of state forest; canoe and camp.
23 acres + butterfly atrium overlooking the park.
265 acres of the country's largest urban zoo; Congo Gorilla Forest is the standout.
15 coasters, a water park & chocolate everything.
Three amusement piers and two waterparks over a beach so wide it needs a tram.
How a Lancaster caramel maker built a company town — with a hands-on chocolate lab.
85 minutes across Delaware Bay with dolphins off the rail; car optional, walk-on works.
250 acres with old-growth forest and a Victorian glasshouse that anchors the whole place.
Pennsylvania's tallest waterfall, three tiers, a short steep walk from the lot.
Eleven acres of North American natives — bison, ocelots, gators — walkable in an afternoon.
Antique autos, a Tucker, and one of the country's great vintage bus collections.
Gifford Pinchot's French chateau above Milford, with a dining table you eat around a moat.
Glacial meltwater drilled a chain of polished rock potholes into Wheelbarrow Run.
Free hawk-watch platform, dune trails, and the wreck of the concrete ship offshore.
199 steps to a 360-degree view over the dune forest where the bay meets the ocean.
A whole town of painted Victorians, plus a walkable pedestrian mall three blocks off the sand.
Guided walk 71 steps down into cool limestone rooms; gem panning and goats up top.
Hedge foxhunt in mid-leap across 22 garden rooms in Maryland horse country.
Free admission, pay-per-ride, wooden coasters, and a haunted house that's a genuine classic.
Highest point on the Palisades cliffs; trails down to the river and a snack bar at the top.
Six miles of Atlantic sand, an Art Deco bathhouse, and a boardwalk built for crowds.
Gothic Revival pile in Tarrytown with Hudson views, a rose garden, and a ruined greenhouse.
Olmsted-designed Scranton park with a gorge, waterfalls, treetop walk, and pools.
Vintage streetcars plus an excursion ride through the Crown Avenue tunnel.
Working roundhouse and turntable full of steam locomotives, run by the Park Service.
Smithsonian-affiliated, 24,000 artifacts, told evenhandedly from both sides of the war.
The immigrant, labor, and hard-coal story of NEPA told through tools, homes, and voices.
Ride a mine car 300 feet down into a real anthracite seam with a retired miner guiding.
A working 18th-century mill farm in Irving's Sleepy Hollow; seasonal, so check the calendar.
A 1906 Beaux-Arts showpiece with a tiled Moravian floor; guided tours are free.
Mastodons, mine cars, and Rothermel's enormous Gettysburg canvas, plus a planetarium.
WWII fire-control towers, a mile-long fishing pier, and dunes where the bay turns ocean.
Free-ish tours of the off-centered flagship, plus a tasting room and steampunk treehouse.
Free admission to the world's largest Matisse collection, plus a sculpture garden.
Beloved no-frills rides, Thrasher's fries, and a mile of Delaware's tidiest boardwalk.
Star-shaped fort where the bombs bursting in air wrote the national anthem.
Free, wildly eclectic collection spanning mummies to armor to Faberge.
Climb aboard the 1854 sloop Constellation, a WWII sub and a lightship.
Four floors of reef, rainforest and rays wrapped around Baltimore's Inner Harbor.

attractions.n: Top of the World Observation Level
attractions.s: 27th-floor 360-degree view over the harbor for the price of a sandwich.
Gloriously strange outsider art in a mirror-clad building on Federal Hill.
Third-oldest US zoo: elephants, penguins and 135 acres inside Druid Hill Park.
Nearly 250 years of Baltimore under one roof; come for the crab cake.
Hands-on physics, dinosaurs and a planetarium right on the harbor promenade.
The ballpark that changed ballparks, with the B&O Warehouse in right field.
Perkins Drive to the summit, a lake loop, and the Hudson gorge spread out below.
America's oldest railroad collection under a vast 1884 roundhouse dome.
The cramped Baltimore rowhouse where Poe wrote his earliest tales.
Falls Trail strings 21 named waterfalls through a hemlock gorge; the state's best hike.
500 acres of rolling field with monumental sculpture; rent a bike, it's bigger than it looks.
Chesapeake beach with the Bay Bridge filling the horizon; swim, crab, kite.
Gray granite fortress academy above the Hudson; guided bus tour only, and bring a Real ID.
Six miles of open coast split by Indian River Inlet — surf, sail, or fish the rocks.

attractions.n: Bethel Woods Center for the Arts
attractions.s: Amphitheater and museum on the actual 1969 Woodstock field in the Sullivan Catskills.
Yard tours, the domed chapel and John Paul Jones's crypt below it.

attractions.n: U.S. Army Heritage and Education Center
attractions.s: Free Carlisle campus with a mile-long outdoor Army Heritage Trail of full-scale exhibits.
Ego Alley, brick streets and sailboats at the foot of colonial Annapolis.
Georgian mansion of a Declaration signer with two terraced pleasure gardens.
Oldest working US statehouse; Washington resigned his commission here.
Working shipyard, floating fleet and a screwpile lighthouse you can climb.
A daylit Nabisco box factory turned minimalist cathedral: Serra, Judd, Flavin at full scale.
6,000 acres of hallowed ground, an auto tour, and the restored Cyclorama painting.
At the AT's halfway point in Pine Grove Furnace State Park — home of the half-gallon challenge.
Ike's only home — a working farm beside the battlefield, tours free from the park shuttle.
Wild horses on an undeveloped barrier island — stay 40 feet back, they bite.

attractions.n: National Museum of Civil War Medicine
attractions.s: Unflinching, superbly told story of battlefield surgery in downtown Frederick.
1873 brick market hall on Capitol Hill; blueberry-buckwheat pancakes inside, flea market outside.

attractions.n: Supreme Court of the United States
attractions.s: Marble temple facing the Capitol; walk in free for a courtroom lecture or live oral arguments.
The Jefferson Building's gilded Great Hall is the most beautiful room in Washington, and free.
Free zoo climbing Rock Creek's slope; giant pandas Bao Li and Qing Bao anchor the Asia Trail.
Free guided tours under the dome; reserve ahead through the visitor center or your congressman.
Presidential portraits and a glass-roofed courtyard, sharing the old Patent Office with SAAM; free.
Voice-activated museum of language in a restored schoolhouse; free, and a sleeper favorite.
Victorian glasshouse at the Capitol's foot: jungle, desert and orchids under one roof; free.
The box where Lincoln was shot, plus the Petersen House across the street where he died.
The only Leonardo in the Americas, plus a neon-lit tunnel between wings.

attractions.n: National Museum of the American Indian
attractions.s: Curved limestone building, Native-curated galleries and the best cafeteria on the Mall; free.
Declaration, Constitution and Bill of Rights in dim light under armored glass; free, timed entry.
Wright Flyer, Apollo 11 capsule and a moon rock you can touch. Free.
Hope Diamond, a T. rex mid-kill and the giant elephant in the rotunda.
America's first modern art museum; Renoir's Boating Party hangs in a Dupont Circle townhouse.
The Smithsonian's concrete doughnut of contemporary art, with a sunken sculpture garden; free.
Tours need a congressional request months out; most visitors settle for the Lafayette Square view.
Neo-Gothic giant finished in 1990, with a Darth Vader gargoyle and a moon rock set in the glass.
Star-Spangled Banner, Julia Child's kitchen, ruby slippers; free and gloriously scattershot.
Bond's Aston Martin, a KGB lipstick pistol and an undercover identity for the day; ticketed.

attractions.n: National Museum of African American History and Culture
attractions.s: Bronze-latticed landmark tracing four centuries from the hold upward.
555-ft obelisk with a 70-second elevator to the best view in the city; free timed tickets go fast.
Harrowing, superbly built permanent exhibition; free, but timed passes are required in peak months.
Granite pillars and fountains between the Monument and Lincoln; free, and best after dark when lit.
Two-mile loop past Jefferson, FDR and MLK, and 3,000 cherry trees that mob it every early April.
Pantheon-style domed rotunda across the Tidal Basin, ringed by cherry trees; free, open 24 hours.
Maya Lin's black granite wall cut into the earth, 58,000 names deep; quiet, free, always open.
Thirty feet of King emerging from the Stone of Hope, facing the Tidal Basin; free, always open.
Four granite outdoor rooms for four terms, with waterfalls and a bronze bread line; free.
Potomac promenade below the C&O Canal and M Street; kayaks, crew shells and cupcake lines.
Riverfront marble slab with a free Millennium Stage show most evenings and a rooftop terrace view.
Nineteen steel soldiers on patrol through juniper; eerie in fog, free, open around the clock.
Nineteen feet of seated marble at the head of the Reflecting Pool.
The Iwo Jima flag-raising cast 32 feet tall with the DC skyline behind it; free, best at sunrise.
624 acres of white headstones, the Tomb of the Unknowns guard change, and JFK's eternal flame.

attractions.n: The Capital Wheel at National Harbor
attractions.s: 180-foot Ferris wheel over the Potomac, with the DC skyline visible on a clear day.
Cobblestoned 18th-century port with King Street shops, waterfront piers and Torpedo Factory studios.
Washington's Potomac plantation: mansion, tomb, working farm and hard truths about the enslaved.

attractions.n: National Museum of the United States Army
attractions.s: Free, ambitious and blessedly uncrowded; tanks, oral histories and a corridor of soldier stories.

events.s: Weekly Saturday market: meats, farm eggs, produce, baked goods, honey, jams.

events.n: Ambler Food Truck Fridays: Cousins Maine Lobster
events.s: Food-truck night at Well Crafted Ambler.
Sidewalk sales and deep discounts at 40+ retailers along Germantown Ave.

events.n: Carnival Family Fun Day — Shops at Valley Square
events.s: Free family carnival in Warrington: bounce houses, blow-up games, face painting, food trucks.
Local artists, makers & musicians at Allen Lane Station, Mt. Airy. Free.
Guided garden walk at the historic Andalusia estate on the Delaware.

events.n: Artes en las Calles — Esperanza Arts Center
events.s: Free summer street celebration with LaGuaGua 47 Orchestra, Brazilian music & food.

events.n: El Centro de Oro Block Party
events.s: Latino cultural block party at 5th & Lehigh: live music and family activities.

events.n: Make The World Better Weekend: Pavement + Kurt Vile
events.s: Pavement + Ratboys (Fri) and Kurt Vile & the Violators (Sat) at the Dell Music Center.
Bucks County's classic ag fair in Wrightstown: 4-H shows, pig races, rides, the famous chicken dinner. Free admission.
Philly's own Jill Scott plays a hometown run at the Met on North Broad.
Free neighborhood jazz festival on the 2200 block of Ridge Ave.
200-ft dragon and massive light displays in Franklin Square; food & performances. $16–29.
National Carousel Day: free cupcakes and buy-one-get-one carousel rides.
Celebration of Jewish culture and history in America's 250th year at the historic Old City congregation.

events.n: RiverRink Summerfest & Spruce St Harbor Park
events.s: Roller skating, rides, mini-golf, hammocks & boardwalk food on the Delaware. Free entry.

events.n: Sips Undersea: Sunset with the Sharks
events.s: Adults-only evening at Adventure Aquarium: shark exhibits, drinks, Delaware River views.
Train headlines the Camden waterfront amphitheater.

events.n: Twilight in the Gardens — Magic Gardens
events.s: Live music among Isaiah Zagar's mosaics on South Street; BYO blanket and snacks.

events.n: Puerto Rican Day Festival — Bristol Wharf
events.s: 52nd annual waterfront festival: Tito Puente Jr. headlines, Danza Fiesta, food trucks & artisan vendors.

events.n: Summer unWINEd Concert Series — Shady Brook Farm
events.s: Live music, food & drink at the Yardley farm; themed Saturday nights.
Wine tastings paired with rice-centric cuisine at Bok in South Philly.

events.n: Peddler's Village: An American Road Trip
events.s: Giant sand sculptures of US landmarks (Statue of Liberty, Rushmore, Golden Gate) across the village.
Interleague home series against the Yankees at Citizens Bank Park.
Long-running classic car show on the New Hope-Solebury HS campus.
63rd edition at Old Pool Farm, Upper Salford: camping, workshops, big folk lineup.
MLS match at Subaru Park on the Chester riverfront.
Dazzling music-synced fountain performances after dark.
America's biggest free music festival — 500+ acts across Bethlehem's Moravian district & SteelStacks.

fishing.s: Small pond in a big Upper Moreland park; panfish, easy family fishing.
Stocked rainbow/brown/palomino trout (spring + Oct); put-and-take; sunfish & rock bass in summer.
Stocked trout + carp; quiet suburban sleeper, bank fishing, line-recycling bins.
Stocked trout section through the state park; deep pools; October extended-season stocking.
Shoreline fishing at the nature center; bass & panfish.
8 miles of stocked trout water through the Wissahickon gorge — the classic Philly fly water.
Smallmouth, largemouth, crappie, carp; bank + canoe; special-reg trout water below the causeway dam.
Manayunk access; smallmouth, channel cats, muskie in an urban setting.
Small lake with fountain; bass & panfish; family-friendly.
Tidal Delaware: stripers, catfish, bass, muskie; boat launch.
165-ac lake; bass, crappie, cats; 24-hr fishing dock, launch, paddle rentals.
Stocked trout in Stony Creek; meadows and paved-trail access.
365-ac lake: walleye, bass, panfish; rentals + full loop trail. Top Bucks fishery.
Fishing pier + creek; very family-friendly Harleysville park.
Warm-water bass/panfish; lily-pad channels; kayak-friendly, nature center on shore.
8 miles of Skippack Creek: stocked trout, smallmouth, sunnies.
Stocked trout spring + fall; holds big bass; electric/unpowered boats only.
Canal + Delaware River access along the towpath; 50-ac pond, 11 river islands.
814-ac, up to 45 ft deep: walleye, hybrid stripers, big bass, crappie; boat rentals.
1,450-ac premier lake: muskie, hybrid stripers, walleye, bass; marinas & launches.

chinese.s: Chinese e-grocery that delivers to Willow Grove — Sichuan pantry, frozen dim sum, fresh produce to the door.
Closest big Asian supermarket (~15 min): produce, live fish counter, Sichuan pantry staples (doubanjiang, huajiao), food court.
Compact Chinese/pan-Asian grocery on Street Rd — quick pantry runs without the drive to Chinatown.
Huge pan-Asian market: Chinese/Korean/SE-Asian aisles, seafood, housewares, food stalls.

chinese.n: Philadelphia Museum of Art — Chinese galleries
chinese.s: A reassembled Beijing palace reception hall and scholar's study.
Traditional Chengdu recipes and real spice — one of Chinatown's newer Sichuan standouts.
Dry pepper fish, northwest-style BBQ skewers & hot pot — open till midnight every night.
Sichuan small plates plus hand-pulled biang biang noodles.
Shanghai-style xiao long bao — the Chinatown soup-dumpling staple.
Classic cart-service Cantonese dim sum; go early on weekends.
Wagyu-focused conveyor-belt hot pot — widely called the best hot pot in the city.
Hong Kong-style bakery: pineapple buns, egg tarts, milk bread.
HK-style mango desserts: sago, pomelo, grass jelly.
Philadelphia Chinatown Development Corporation — the community org behind Chinatown's festivals; their calendar lists everything happening.
Nightly thru 8/16 in Franklin Square — giant lanterns handmade by Zigong artisans from Sichuan.

chinese.n: Chengdu Famous Food 成都名小吃
chinese.s: Casual Chengdu snack shop: dan dan mian, chuan chuan skewers, sweet-water noodles — closest thing to a Chengdu xiaochi stall.

chinese.n: Nan Zhou Hand Drawn Noodle 兰州拉面
chinese.s: Hand-pulled Lanzhou beef noodle soup, made in the window.

chinese.n: Sang Kee Peking Duck House 北京烤鸭
chinese.s: 40+ years of roast duck, wonton noodle soup & salt-baked squid.
Elegant Asian patisserie: airy cream cakes, matcha, taro, mango.

chinese.n: Ten Ren Tea 天仁茗茶
chinese.s: Taiwanese tea institution: loose-leaf teas + fresh bubble tea.
The heart of it all: the 10th & Arch Friendship Gate, BBQ shops, herbal stores, bakeries — make a day of it.
30th annual street festival: lion & dragon dances, mooncake contest, opera, kids' lantern parade. Mid-Autumn 2026 falls Fri Sept 25 — festival on the nearby weekend.

chinese.n: Lunar New Year in Philadelphia 春节
chinese.s: Lion dances, parades & banquets across Chinatown and the city every February (next: Feb 6, 2027).
Chef Zhao's Chinatown institution — Philly's benchmark Sichuan: mapo tofu, water-boiled fish, dry pot, proper má-là.
The famous dan dan noodles & dry pepper-style chicken; several area locations.
Stylish Sichuan on Chestnut St — peppercorn classics in a date-night room.
Sichuan–Taiwanese favorite near Rittenhouse Square; second location in Ardmore.
One of the finest Chinese galleries in America: Tang horses & the famous crystal sphere under a 90-ft dome.
Big Viet-Chinese market on Washington Ave: produce, fresh noodles, roast meats nearby.
Beloved XLB & dumpling house out in Media — handy for west-suburb trips.
